title: Instance System Nodes
description: Implement 5 instancing nodes that enable placing copies of geometry at many points efficiently. This is critical for procedural modeling -- e.g., placing fingers on a hand, scales on a fish, teeth in a mouth. InstanceOnPoints: place an instance of geometry at each point with optional rotation/scale per point. GeometryToInstance: convert geometry to an instance reference (lightweight handle, not duplicated data). RealizeInstances: flatten all instances into real geometry (expand references into actual vertices/faces). ScaleInstances: scale each instance around its own pivot. SplitToInstances: break a mesh into connected components, each becoming an instance.
definition-of-done: 1. Five node classes in nodes/instances/. 2. Instance concept defined: a reference to source geometry + transform (position, rotation, scale). 3. InstanceOnPoints places one instance per input point with per-point transform. 4. RealizeInstances produces a HalfEdgeMesh with all instance geometry merged. 5. ScaleInstances scales each instance independently. 6. SplitToInstances creates separate instances from disconnected mesh components. 7. GeometryToInstance wraps a mesh as an instance reference.
testing-plan: 1. InstanceOnPoints: place a cube at 4 corner positions, RealizeInstances, verify 4 * 8 = 32 vertices. 2. ScaleInstances by 2x, realize, verify vertex positions doubled from each instance center. 3. SplitToInstances on two disjoint cubes, verify 2 instances. 4. GeometryToInstance -> InstanceOnPoints round-trip with a single point, verify identical geometry after realize.
todos: Define Instance class with source geometry reference, position, rotation, scale
Define InstanceCollection as a container of instances
Implement RealizeInstancesNode: merge all instances into one HalfEdgeMesh
Implement SplitToInstancesNode using connected component detection
unknowns: Whether instances should support nested instancing (instance of instances)
How to handle instance attributes (e.g., per-instance color)
